channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Woodside may exercise veto over Inpex acquisition of Browse gas fields
item.description: Woodside Energy is evaluating whether to use its pre-emptive rights after Japan’s Inpex agreed to purchase PetroChina’s 10.67% stake in three undeveloped Browse gas fields: Calliance, Brecknock, and T...

item.title: Ontario family joins volunteer search for missing man in Mont-Tremblant
item.description: The family of Liam Toman, an Ontario man who went missing in February 2025 during a ski trip in Mont-Tremblant, Quebec, is participating in a spring search this weekend. The search involves volunteer ...

item.title: Persistent ghost worker issue highlights governance failures in public works
item.description: The Sunday Times has revealed that the Department of Public Works has lost over R14 million to ghost workers, with the majority of cases in the Durban regional office. The phenomenon, where salaries a...

item.title: Global Companies Face $25 Billion Losses Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: The ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran has already caused massive financial strain on companies worldwide, totalling at least $25 billion and the figure is still increasing, according to Reuters analysis....

item.title: NSW’s decision to bring back James Tedesco for State of Origin praised
item.description: James Tedesco, at 33, is set to return to the New South Wales Blues for State of Origin after missing last year’s selection. Despite strong performances from younger players like Dylan Edwards, Tedesc...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Week 20 2026: Equity Turnover Reaches TZS 28.09 Billion
item.description: During Week 20 of 2026, from 11th to 15th May, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange recorded strong activity in equity trading. Equity turnover excluding ETF trades climbed by 48.06 percent to TZS 28.09 b...

item.title: Saskatoon Fire Department Reports Significant Increase in Overdose and Encampment Calls
item.description: In 2025, the Saskatoon Fire Department experienced a dramatic increase in demands on its services, largely due to the surge in overdoses and encampment-related incidents across the city. The departmen...

item.title: Prominent Tribal Elder Killed in Wana IED Blast, Three Others Injured
item.description: In a tragic incident on Monday morning in Wana, Lower South Waziristan, an improvised explosive device (IED) exploded in Rustam Bazaar, killing three people including Malik Tariq Wazir, a respected tr...

item.title: World Bank Did Not Participate in VP Sara Duterte’s Impeachment Hearings, Video Claim False
item.description: A viral YouTube video claimed that the World Bank had testified in the impeachment proceedings against Vice President Sara Duterte. Rappler’s fact check confirms that this claim is false. The misleadi...

item.title: Yvette Cooper publishes article despite warnings it could influence Palestine Action trial
item.description: Yvette Cooper, then Home Secretary, published an article in The Observer in August last year discussing the proscription of Palestine Action, despite being warned by the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS...

item.title: Exploring the History and Impact of Photography in Children's Books
item.description: The exhibition “L is for Look,” currently on view at Lausanne’s Photo Elysée, explores the rich history of children’s photobooks and their role in education, storytelling, and visual literacy. Beginni...

item.title: KP CM Requests Immediate Government Action to Resolve CNG Shortage
item.description: PESHAWAR – Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi has formally requested Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif's intervention over the ongoing CNG shortage in the province, signalling potential unres...

item.title: Human-Caused Wildfire on Santa Rosa Island Burns Over 10,000 Acres, Threatening Unique Species and Forcing Evacuations
item.description: A human-caused wildfire rapidly expanded on Santa Rosa Island, the second-largest of California's Channel Islands, burning more than 10,000 acres as of Sunday night, May 17, 2026. The fire, first spot...

item.title: Naira holds steady against dollar on May 18, 2026, with slight market fluctuations
item.description: On Monday, May 18, 2026, the Nigerian naira continued to show minor fluctuations against the US dollar in both official and parallel markets, as demand for foreign exchange remained strong. According ...

item.title: India expresses concern over drone strike near UAE's Barakah nuclear plant, calls for restraint and diplomacy
item.description: India has conveyed serious concern regarding the recent drone strike near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the UAE, terming the attack as unacceptable and a dangerous escalation in the region. The M...

item.title: Ramaphosa, Phala Phala, and the Politics of Consistent Constitutional Accountability
item.description: The recent Constitutional Court ruling on Parliament's handling of the Phala Phala matter has sparked a wave of calls for President Cyril Ramaphosa's resignation, primarily from the MK Party, EFF, and...

item.title: Trump’s China Visit Highlights U.S. Economic and Corporate Pressures
item.description: Donald Trump's visit to Beijing in May 2026 has been portrayed as more than a standard diplomatic mission. The article suggests that Trump traveled not as the leader of a confident superpower, but as ...

item.title: Calls for Special Investigating Unit to probe alleged R2 billion Ekurhuleni billing fraud
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has formally requested President Cyril Ramaphosa to authorise a Special Investigating Unit (SIU) probe into a massive R2 billion billing scandal in Ekurhuleni, which is al...

item.title: Jennifer Jones’ Iconic Performance in 'Portrait of Jennie' (1948) Captured in Photos
item.description: The 1948 film 'Portrait of Jennie' showcased Jennifer Jones in a role widely celebrated for its ethereal and haunting quality. She played Jennie Appleton, a mysterious girl who appears to a struggling...

item.title: Yanga Prepares for Challenging Season to Defend Premier League Crown
item.description: Yanga Sports Club is set to face a challenging season as dey try to defend their Tanzanian Premier League title. After last season’s success, fans and management are hopeful but aware dat competition ...
